title: Eragrostis elegantula
taxonomy: Eragrostis elegantula is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Eragrostis, which contains over 200 species of grasses worldwide. Eragrostis elegantula is native to tropical Africa and is commonly found in savannas and grasslands. As a member of the Poaceae family, it is a monocotyledonous plant, meaning it has one cotyledon or seed leaf in its seedling stage. Eragrostis elegantula is further classified as a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a subfamily of the grass family Poaceae.

intro: Eragrostis elegantula, also known as sweet grass or buffalo grass, is a perennial grass native to North America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The health benefits of Eragrostis elegantula include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eragrostis elegantula are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
